zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Norrish於1573年出生在英國戴文易亞斯特 阿林頓時，她的父親Nicholas Norrishe20歲，母親Elizabeth Sloute21歲。她於1605年6月與Philip Bunker在英國戴文易亞斯特 阿林頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1644年6月在她的家鄉享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: Norrish
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: variant of Norris . For the development of final -s to -sh compare Morrish and Parish . There may have been some confusion with Nurrish

variants.name: Norris
meanings: habitational name from Middle English norreis, noreis, norais ‘northerner’ (Anglo-Norman French noreis), used both of Norwegians and of inhabitants of northern England and Scotland.
from the Middle English personal name Norreis, derived from norreis ‘northerner’.

variants.name: Morrish
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Morris  . Compare   Parrish  .

variants.name: Nurrish
meanings: English: see Nurse . For the development of final -s to -sh compare Morrish and Parish . There may have been some confusion with Norrish .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Norrish was born in 1573, in East Allington, Devon, England, her father, Nicholas Norrishe, was 20 and her mother, Elizabeth Sloute, was 21. She married Philip Bunker in June 1605, in East Allington, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in June 1644, in her hometown, at the age of 71.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Norrish(이)가 1573년 영국 데폰 이스트 앨링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Norrishe(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Sloute(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1605년 6월 영국 데폰 이스트 앨링턴에서 Philip Bunker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1644년 6월 그녀의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Norrish родилась в 1573 г. в местности эаст аллингтон, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Norrishe было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Sloute, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip Bunker июнь 1605 г. в местности эаст аллингтон, девон, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла июнь 1644 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 71 год.
